title: Red Hat Security Advisory: pcs security update
overview:
An update for pcs is now available for Red Hat Enterprise Linux 10.0 Extended Update Support.

Red Hat Product Security has rated this update as having a security impact of Important. A Common Vulnerability Scoring System (CVSS) base score, which gives a detailed severity rating, is available for each vulnerability from the CVE link(s) in the References section.

affected_products: Red Hat Enterprise Linux High Availability EUS (v. 10.0)
full_text:
Red Hat Security Advisory: pcs security update

An update for pcs is now available for Red Hat Enterprise Linux 10.0 Extended Update Support.

Red Hat Product Security has rated this update as having a security impact of Important. A Common Vulnerability Scoring System (CVSS) base score, which gives a detailed severity rating, is available for each vulnerability from the CVE link(s) in the References section.

An update for pcs is now available for Red Hat Enterprise Linux 10.0 Extended Update Support.

Red Hat Product Security has rated this update as having a security impact of Important. A Common Vulnerability Scoring System (CVSS) base score, which gives a detailed severity rating, is available for each vulnerability from the CVE link(s) in the References section.

Updated Packages:
pcs-0.12.0-3.el10_0.4.src.rpm
cockpit-ha-cluster-0.12.0-3.el10_0.4.noarch.rpm
pcs-0.12.0-3.el10_0.4.x86_64.rpm
pcs-snmp-0.12.0-3.el10_0.4.x86_64.rpm
pcs-0.12.0-3.el10_0.4.ppc64le.rpm
pcs-snmp-0.12.0-3.el10_0.4.ppc64le.rpm
pcs-0.12.0-3.el10_0.4.s390x.rpm
pcs-snmp-0.12.0-3.el10_0.4.s390x.rpm
pcs-0.12.0-3.el10_0.4.aarch64.rpm
pcs-snmp-0.12.0-3.el10_0.4.aarch64.rpm

CVE-2025-13465: A flaw was found in Lodash. A prototype pollution vulnerability in the _.unset and _.omit functions allows an attacker able to control property paths to delete methods from global prototypes. By removing essential functionalities, this can result in a denial of service.
